claim: Retrospective failure attribution lacks a Process Gap category for orchestrator process failures
actual: implementation-guide.md lines 211-217 list only Spec gap, Compilation gap, and Implementation error. Phase 1 and Phase 2 retros document orchestrator skipping go test ./... which fits none of these. Intermediate retro confirms at line 61.

claim: feature-spec-guide Section 5 does not require verifying testable output before listing a test
actual: Lines 33-43 require stating what behavior a test validates but never check whether the code path produces assertable output. Phase 0 AC-03 and AC-06 were accepted gaps because functions only produced log output. Intermediate retro line 78 confirms spec author did not verify observability.

claim: spec-design-thinking testability section checks callability but not assertability of output
actual: Lines 7-11 ask whether a test can import and call the function with known inputs, but do not check whether the function returns a value or produces observable state change that can be asserted on. Phase 0 AC-03/AC-06 were callable but produced only log output.

claim: Add always-nil feature parameters checklist item to ai-failure-modes.md
actual: File has 11 items, none covering always-nil parameters. Phase 3 CR-F02 confirms the retro source. Gap is real and non-redundant.

claim: task-compilation.md has no explicit prohibition on test tasks modifying production source files
actual: Lines 159-169 define test tasks as writing tests and impl tasks as writing code, but no constraint prevents test tasks from modifying production files. Phase 0 retro lines 67 and 133 confirm task-14 added production accessor methods to interfaces.go from a test task.

claim: Add semantically incoherent test fixtures checklist item to ai-failure-modes.md
actual: File has 11 items, none covering fixture internal consistency. Phase 0 retro confirms 3 findings of store/candidate type mismatch. Gap is real.

claim: Add fixture consistency section to test-authoring.md
actual: File covers production-path exercise, re-implementation, self-assignment, isolation, assertion specificity, test names. No section addresses cross-object fixture field consistency. Gap is real.

claim: Add zero-value fixture fields section to test-authoring.md
actual: Assertion specificity section covers assertion values, not fixture construction. No instruction to explicitly set every field production code reads. Phase 0 retro confirms zero-value NodeKind finding.

claim: Add fixture consistency check to test-reviewer CP3
actual: CP3 checks traceability, compilation, task matching, behavioral testing, skipped tests, phantom assertions. No check for internally inconsistent fixture objects. Gap is real.

claim: Add internal fixture consistency check to detector sighting output
actual: Detector sighting output section defines format and classification but has no detection target for fixture consistency in test-integrity sightings. Gap is real.

claim: Add test fixture coherence verification step to challenger
actual: Verification protocol covers general verification, caller tracing, pending-execution, nit rejection, and adjacent observations. No fixture-specific verification guidance. Gap is real but narrower than presented -- general verification protocol already applies.
notes: General verification protocol partially covers this; proposal adds fixture-specific concreteness

claim: Instruct test reviewer to flag fixture type mismatches at spec review
actual: Test strategy review in SKILL.md invokes test reviewer at CP1 with spec file. At CP1 no fixtures exist -- fixtures are authored later. Instruction to flag fixture type mismatches is premature at this stage. Should be reframed to strategy-level guidance or deferred to CP3 (already covered by P-31).
notes: Wrong pipeline stage. At spec review, fixtures do not exist. Reframe to strategy-level or rely on P-31 at CP3.

claim: Add fixture field consistency check to Guardian review prompt
actual: Guardian prompt reviews testing strategy at spec time. Fixture objects do not exist during spec review. Instruction to verify fixture field values is inapplicable at this pipeline stage. Must be reframed to strategy-level guidance.
notes: Wrong pipeline stage. Guardian reviews at spec time; fixtures are authored later.

claim: Add consistency constraints instruction to agents.md Instruction Design
actual: Instruction Design section covers role/scope, workflow/checklist, description writing. No guidance on stating consistency constraints between related instruction parts. Phase 0 retro confirms agents followed per-field instructions literally without cross-object consistency. Gap is real and correctly generalized.

claim: Add all-instances scan section to quality-detection.md
actual: File has no instruction about scanning for all instances of a detected pattern. Gap is real. However, this proposal is MISCATEGORIZED -- it belongs in Theme H (Same-File Pattern Instance Propagation) not Theme C (Test Fixture Semantic Consistency). The retro source is about Round 1 fixing one fixture mismatch but missing identical mismatch in same file, which is a pattern-propagation concern.
notes: Miscategorized under Theme C. Belongs in Theme H per retro source and proposal description.

claim: Add detection-source breakdown to code-review-guide.md Post-output step 1
actual: Post-output step 1 (line 104) lists finding count, rejection count, false positive rate but omits detection-source breakdown. Retrospective Fields section (line 121) mentions it but the actionable step does not. Gap is real.
notes: Retrospective Fields section partially covers this but the operational instruction in Post-output step 1 does not include it

claim: fbk-breakdown SKILL.md test task agent section does not restrict file scope to test files
actual: Lines 29-37 say test tasks specify files to create without constraining them to test files only. Same retro evidence as proposal 65 applies - task-14 created production accessor methods from a test task.

claim: Add escalation rule to context-assets.md Choose the Right Trigger section
actual: The Choose the Right Trigger section (lines 56-91) has no instruction about escalating from documented rules to hooks when rules fail repeatedly. Intermediate retro (lines 61-62) explicitly calls for this. Gap is real.

claim: Add corrective action escalation instruction to hooks.md Command Design section
actual: Command Design section (lines 73-84) covers speed, deterministic checks, script extraction, but has no guidance on when to escalate a recurring corrective action to a hook. Gap is real.

claim: Add post-extraction duplicate cleanup instruction to function-design.md Function scope
actual: Function scope section (lines 29-32) covers extraction and composition but says nothing about removing duplicate constructions from callers after extraction. Phase 3 CR-F01 confirms agents left duplicates. Gap is real.

claim: Impl tasks must be in strictly later wave than paired test tasks
actual: task-compilation.md line 167 explicitly ALLOWS same-wave placement: Test tasks and implementation tasks in the same wave may run in parallel. The Phase 1 retro line 102 says the gate requires strict wave precedence but this mischaracterizes the current rule. The practical fix (moving tests to wave 0) supports the change, but the proposal is changing an existing allowance, not closing a gap. The edit must remove the same-wave permission at line 167, not just add a new constraint.
notes: The retro source is slightly inaccurate - it claims the gate requires strict wave precedence, but the current text explicitly allows same-wave. The proposal should be framed as tightening an existing rule rather than fixing a violation. The edit must also remove or revise the contradicting text at line 167.

claim: Add identical argument construction dedup to composition-patterns.md Composition as testable unit
actual: The gap is real — no existing instruction covers consolidating identical argument construction. However, the target section (Composition as testable unit, lines 28-32) is about testability of composition logic, not about deduplication cleanup. The instruction is valid but placement is mismatched with the section purpose.
notes: Consider placing under a new section or under Caller owns side effects instead of Composition as testable unit

claim: Change hook warning from ask whether to proceed to stop and tell the user in fbk-implement/SKILL.md
actual: Line 41 currently says Ask whether to proceed. The retro source (intermediate retro lines 61-62, Phase 2 retro lines 90-94) documents that soft gates get bypassed. The current text is a soft gate pattern. Gap is real.
notes: Applied by analogy from final-verification skipping to hook-missing warning

claim: Add run final verification before writing the retrospective to fbk-implement/SKILL.md Step 6
actual: The sequencing constraint already exists. Line 69 says Step 6 Final wave only: After the checkpoint, proceed to final verification. Line 89 says Do not write the retrospective until final verification passes. The proposed instruction is redundant with existing content.
notes: Both the Step 6 directive and the Final Verification section already enforce this sequencing

claim: task-compilation.md Codebase-Grounded Compilation section lacks instruction to grep all call sites of changed symbols
actual: Section exists at lines 7-16. It says to read actual files and compile spec-identified impacts, but only processes impacts the spec already enumerates. No instruction to independently grep for all call sites of any changed symbol. Retros confirm this was the dominant failure mode across 3 phases.

claim: fbk-breakdown/SKILL.md Implementation task agent section lacks all callers of any changed symbol in file path enumeration
actual: Implementation task agent section (lines 41-49) specifies files to create/modify with explicit paths but does not instruct the agent to grep for all callers of changed symbols. The gap is real.

claim: brownfield-breakdown.md lacks instruction to grep all references including test files before declaring task scope
actual: File has 5 instructions across 10 lines. Instruction 1 says search the codebase for related functionality but does not specifically mention test files or call-site references. No instruction mentions grepping all references. The gap is real.

claim: brownfield-breakdown.md instruction 1 should expand existing files to include test files and config packages
actual: Instruction 1 (line 1) says Search the codebase for related functionality. Line 3 says Each task that modifies existing code must reference files by path. Neither mentions existing files verbatim as the proposal claims. The gap is real -- test files and config packages are not mentioned -- but the proposal description misquotes the text as existing files when the actual text is related functionality and existing code.
notes: Minor description inaccuracy: proposal says expand existing files but the actual text uses different phrasing

claim: brownfield-spec.md instruction 4 (replacement scope) should add grep for all references before declaring removal scope
actual: Instruction 4 (line 7) says If the feature replaces existing functionality include removal or migration of the old path in scope. No instruction to grep for all references. Gap is real. However, report Issue 1 confirms this file has no explicit routing -- no skill or guide loads it. Proposals 18-19 improve content but have no effect until a routing fix is applied.
notes: Gap is real but file is unreachable by deterministic routing per report Issue 1. Effectiveness depends on routing fix.

claim: brownfield-spec.md after instruction 1 should add search all call sites for signature changes
actual: Instruction 1 (line 1) says Search the codebase for existing code that overlaps with the proposed feature. No mention of call sites for signature changes. Gap is real. Same routing caveat as P-18: file has no explicit routing from any skill.
notes: Gap is real but file is unreachable by deterministic routing per report Issue 1

claim: feature-spec-guide.md Section 5b should add grep for all call sites of removed/renamed symbols in test files
actual: Section 5b (line 35) already says search the test suite for tests that cover the files and functions this feature modifies. This is close to the proposal but does not specifically mention call-site completeness for removed or renamed symbols. Gap is narrower than described -- partial coverage exists.
notes: Existing text partially addresses this. The addition should focus on removed/renamed symbol call sites specifically, not search the test suite generically which is already there.

claim: fbk-spec/SKILL.md Gate section lacks check that testing strategy enumerates all callers
actual: Gate section (lines 31-46) runs a gate script and presents semantic criteria. No check for caller enumeration completeness. The gap is real.

claim: spec-review/SKILL.md Council invocation should instruct Architect to verify call-site completeness
actual: Council invocation section (lines 35-39) frames prompts using SDL concerns table. The Architect prompt in review-perspectives.md covers integration risks and pattern consistency but not call-site completeness for symbol changes. The gap is real.

claim: spec-review/SKILL.md Finding synthesis should classify missing call site as blocking finding
actual: Finding synthesis section (lines 41-45) tags findings with severity but has no specific rule about missing call sites being blocking. The gap is real.

claim: review-perspectives.md Guardian prompt should verify impacted tests list enumerates all call sites
actual: Guardian prompt (line 27) already says Verify that impacted existing tests are identified -- search the test suite for coverage of affected files and functions. This partially addresses the concern but does not specifically say enumerate all call sites in test files. Gap is narrower than described.
notes: Existing Guardian prompt already includes search the test suite for coverage of affected files and functions. The proposal should sharpen this to call-site enumeration specifically, not add a wholly new instruction.

claim: sdl-workflow.md Stage Guides routing table should add call-site grep before declaring task file scope
actual: Stage Guides section (lines 26-44) is a routing table mapping stages to skill/doc paths. It contains no per-stage process instructions beyond routing. Adding a call-site grep instruction here is a content-type mismatch -- this section routes to guides, it does not contain process steps. The gap is real but the proposed location is wrong. The instruction belongs in task-compilation.md (P-14) or implementation-guide.md.
notes: Routing table is the wrong location for this instruction. P-14 already covers the same gap in the correct file.

claim: test-reviewer.md CP2 should verify test tasks enumerate all call sites of changed functions
actual: CP2 (lines 95-107) verifies test task fidelity to the testing strategy and UV step coverage. It does not verify that test tasks enumerate all call sites of changed functions. The gap is real -- the Phase 2 post-wave fix was caused exactly by a missed call site in a test file that CP2 did not catch.

claim: agents.md Instruction Design section should instruct agents to search all call sites before writing code
actual: Instruction Design section (lines 73-99) covers agent body structure, workflow checklists, and description writing. It is a meta-guide about how to define agents, not an operational guide about what agents should do during implementation. Adding search all call sites here would be oddly placed -- this instruction belongs in the operational guides that agents consume (task-compilation.md, implementation-guide.md), not in the agent definition authoring guide.
notes: Wrong target file. agents.md teaches how to write agent definitions, not what agents should do during coding. The instruction belongs in operational guides like task-compilation.md or implementation-guide.md.

claim: detector.md Do not write files redundant with tool restriction
actual: Frontmatter tools: Read, Grep, Glob excludes Write/Edit/Bash. Line 16 restates this as prose.

claim: test-reviewer.md CP5 hard-codes test-hash-gate.sh and verify.yml
actual: Line 151 refs test-hash-gate.sh, line 157 refs verify.yml. Both project-specific in general agent.

claim: post-impl-review.md convergence uses nit-category vs code-review-guide info
actual: post-impl-review.md:23 says nit-category. code-review-guide.md:99 says no new sightings above info. Nits excluded from classification (line 87-88).

claim: post-impl-review.md Output has double prohibition
actual: Line 28 has two Do not sentences. Authoring rules require positive framing with prohibitions.

claim: brownfield-breakdown.md instruction 2 is compound
actual: Line 2 has two constraints: modify-existing refs by path AND new-file justification. Authoring rules require single constraint per instruction.

claim: fbk-council SKILL.md Logger Commands should be extracted
actual: Logger Commands Reference runs lines 726-791 (~66 lines). SKILL.md is 985 lines vs 500-line cap in skills.md:50.

claim: fbk-council SKILL.md Ralph Wiggum section should be extracted
actual: Ralph section runs lines 812-985 (173 lines). Even after extracting both 81+82, file would be ~746 lines, still over 500 cap.
notes: Extracting both proposals still leaves file over 500-line cap

claim: config-yml-schema model field should enumerate valid per-stage keys
actual: Line 8 says plus per-stage keys but does not list them. Example shows reviewing/implementing but examples are not normative.

claim: threat-modeling.md Step 2 should verify spec claims against source
actual: Step 2 (line 42) reads spec only. Does not instruct verifying spec claims match actual code.

claim: threat-modeling.md Step 4 should exclude dead code from asset enumeration
actual: Step 4 (line 46) enumerates assets without distinguishing active from dead code. Dead code does not expose real attack surfaces.
